title: RV8GR store/load/branch trace
purpose: Package the SB, LB, and BEQ T2 traces so store direction, load direction, branch loading, bus ownership, and architectural state can be checked without running the full CPU.

chips.role: DBUS/IBUS bridge; DBUS-to-IBUS for load, IBUS-to-DBUS for store
AC output buffer for SB store data
immediate operand driver for BEQ target low byte
RAM byte at {DP,IRL} for SB and LB

wiring.rule: SB and LB select {DP,IRL}; BEQ keeps ABUS on PC during T2.
1 means IBUS to DBUS for store; 0 means DBUS to IBUS for load.
LOW for LB and SB T2 data movement.
LOW only when branch/jump control takes PC in T2.

behavior.sb: Trace 2: opcode 0x04 drives AC through U14 onto IBUS, U7 writes DBUS, and RAM[{DP,IRL}] captures AC when A15 selects RAM.
behavior.lb: Trace 7: opcode 0x38 selects RAM[{DP,IRL}], U7 bridges DBUS to IBUS, and AC captures the RAM byte.
behavior.beq: Trace 4: opcode 0x02 loads PC with {PG,IRL} when Z=1; AC and RAM are unchanged.
behavior.bus_policy: Each vector must have no more than one active IBUS driver and no more than one active DBUS driver.
behavior.edge_policy: The U1_U4 counter/load chain changes PC only on the intended rising edge; opposite edges hold for these trace rows.

timing.frequency_boundary: Functional trace proof only; physical timing is owned by timing_margins.json.
timing.deadband_boundary: SB and LB still require DBUS/IBUS output-float deadband, R/C settling, and destination setup/hold evidence before physical speed signoff.
timing.student_rule: Check bus ownership before checking the state result, because a correct-looking byte is not acceptable if two chips fought for the bus.

guarantees: SB writes AC to RAM at {DP,IRL}
LB reads RAM at {DP,IRL} into AC and updates Z
BEQ loads PC with {PG,IRL} only when Z=1
AC, PC, and RAM final state match each trace vector
IBUS and DBUS ownership is conflict-free for every vector

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
